F, title III, § 3006136 Stat. 2159Sept. 30, 2022“Notwithstanding the amendments made by this title [amending sections 379j–42 and 379j–43 of this title and repealing provisions set out as notes under this section and ], part 7 of subchapter C of chapter VII of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ( et seq.), as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this title [], shall continue to be in effect with respect to abbreviated new drug applications (as defined in such part as of such day) that were received by the Food and Drug Administration within the meaning of section 505(j)(5)(A) of such Act (), prior approval supplements that were submitted, and drug master files for Type II active pharmaceutical ingredients that were first referenced on or after , but before , with respect to assessing and collecting any fee required by such part for a fiscal year prior to fiscal year 2023.”section 379j–43 of this title21 U.S.C. 379j–4121 U.S.C. 355(j)(5)(A)Sept. 30, 2022October 1, 2017October 1, 2022, , , provided that:Pub. children.label: A
children.text: means an application submitted under , an abbreviated application submitted under (as in effect on the day before ), or an abbreviated new drug application submitted pursuant to regulations in effect prior to the implementation of the Drug Price Competition and Patent Term Restoration Act of 1984; andsection 355(j) of this titlesection 357 of this titleNovember 21, 1997

children.label: i
children.text: for a positron emission tomography drug; or

children.label: ii
children.text: submitted by a State or Federal governmental entity for a drug that is not distributed commercially.

children.label: i
children.text: to be used as a component of a drug; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to furnish pharmacological activity or other direct effect in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, or to affect the structure or any function of the human body; or

children.label: B
children.text: a substance intended for final crystallization, purification, or salt formation, or any combination of those activities, to become a substance or mixture described in subparagraph (A).

The term “adjustment factor” means a factor applicable to a fiscal year that is the Consumer Price Index for all urban consumers (all items; United States city average) for October of the preceding fiscal year divided by such Index for October 2011.

children.label: A
children.text: one business entity controls, or has the power to control, the other business entity; or

children.label: B
children.text: a third party controls, or has power to control, both of the business entities.

The term “contract manufacturing organization facility” means a manufacturing facility of a finished dosage form of a drug approved pursuant to an abbreviated new drug application, where such manufacturing facility is not identified in an approved abbreviated new drug application held by the owner of such facility or an affiliate of such owner or facility.

children.label: I
children.text: under one management, either direct or indirect; and

children.label: II
children.text: at one geographic location or address engaged in manufacturing or processing an active pharmaceutical ingredient or a finished dosage form; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not include a business or other entity whose only manufacturing or processing activities are one or more of the following: repackaging, relabeling, or testing.

children.label: i
children.text: closely related to the same business enterprise;

children.label: ii
children.text: under the supervision of the same local management; and

children.label: iii
children.text: capable of being inspected by the Food and Drug Administration during a single inspection.

children.label: C
children.text: If a business or other entity would meet the definition of a facility under this paragraph but for being under multiple management, the business or other entity is deemed to constitute multiple facilities, one per management entity, for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: a drug product in the form in which it will be administered to a patient, such as a tablet, capsule, solution, or topical application;

children.label: B
children.text: a drug product in a form in which reconstitution is necessary prior to administration to a patient, such as oral suspensions or lyophilized powders; or

children.label: C
children.text: any combination of an active pharmaceutical ingredient with another component of a drug product for purposes of production of a drug product described in subparagraph (A) or (B).

The term “generic drug submission” means an abbreviated new drug application, an amendment to an abbreviated new drug application, or a prior approval supplement to an abbreviated new drug application.

children.label: A
children.text: The activities necessary for the review of generic drug submissions, including review of drug master files referenced in such submissions.

children.label: i
children.text: approval letters which approve abbreviated new drug applications or supplements to such applications; or

children.label: ii
children.text: complete response letters which set forth in detail the specific deficiencies in such applications and, where appropriate, the actions necessary to place such applications in condition for approval.

children.label: i
children.text: set forth in detail the specific deficiencies in such submissions, and where appropriate, the actions necessary to resolve those deficiencies; or

children.label: ii
children.text: document that no deficiencies need to be addressed.

children.label: D
children.text: Inspections related to generic drugs.

children.label: E
children.text: Monitoring of research conducted in connection with the review of generic drug submissions and drug master files.

children.label: i
children.text: Collecting, developing, and reviewing safety information on approved drugs, including adverse event reports.

children.label: ii
children.text: Developing and using improved adverse-event data-collection systems, including information technology systems.

children.label: iii
children.text: Developing and using improved analytical tools to assess potential safety problems, including access to external data bases.

children.label: iv
children.text: Implementing and enforcing section 355() of this title (relating to postapproval studies and clinical trials and labeling changes) and (relating to risk evaluation and mitigation strategies) insofar as those activities relate to abbreviated new drug applications.osection 355(p) of this title

children.label: v
children.text: Carrying out (relating to adverse-event reports and postmarket safety activities).section 355(k)(5) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: Regulatory science activities related to generic drugs.

The term “positron emission tomography drug” has the meaning given to the term “compounded positron emission tomography drug” in , except that paragraph (1)(B) of such section shall not apply.section 321(ii) of this title

The term “prior approval supplement” means a request to the Secretary to approve a change in the drug substance, drug product, production process, quality controls, equipment, or facilities covered by an approved abbreviated new drug application when that change has a substantial potential to have an adverse effect on the identity, strength, quality, purity, or potency of the drug product as these factors may relate to the safety or effectiveness of the drug product.

children.label: A
children.text: officers and employees of the Food and Drug Administration, contractors of the Food and Drug Administration, advisory committees, and costs related to such officers and employees and to contracts with such contractors;

children.label: B
children.text: management of information, and the acquisition, maintenance, and repair of computer resources;

children.label: C
children.text: leasing, maintenance, renovation, and repair of facilities and acquisition, maintenance, and repair of fixtures, furniture, scientific equipment, and other necessary materials and supplies; and

children.label: D
children.text: collecting fees under subsection (a) and accounting for resources allocated for the review of abbreviated new drug applications and supplements and inspection related to generic drugs.

The term “Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file” means a submission of information to the Secretary by a person that intends to authorize the Food and Drug Administration to reference the information to support approval of a generic drug submission without the submitter having to disclose the information to the generic drug submission applicant.
